items.title: What to Do in LA if You’re Here for Business (2025)
items.summary: A tech industry guide to where to stay, eat, work, and play while visiting LA.

items.title: ‘Creepy’ Listening Tool for Targeted Ads Didn’t Actually Work, FTC Says
items.summary: Three firms will pay nearly $1 million for selling “Active Listening” technology that they claimed tapped people’s phones for advertising. The FTC alleges the “tech” was just pricey email lists.

items.title: Meta Is in Crisis, Google Search’s Makeover, and AI Gets Booed by Graduates
items.summary: In this episode of Uncanny Valley, we unpack the mass layoffs at Meta, big announcements at Google I/O, and the latest backlash against AI.

items.title: Who will benefit most from SpaceX IPO? Mostly Elon — and a few from his inner circle
items.summary: Elon Musk has the largest stake in SpaceX by billions of shares. The other biggest shareholders also have longstanding and deep ties to Musk.

items.title: Waymo halts freeway rides after robotaxis struggle in construction zones
items.summary: The service suspension comes as Waymo has also paused operations in Atlanta and San Antonio after its robotaxis drove into flooded streets.

items.title: NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani takes to Twitch to chat with New Yorkers
items.summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani is launching a Twitch series today at 4 p.m. ET.

items.title: Rubio says Cuba is threat to US as Havana accuses him of 'lies'
items.summary: Cuba's foreign minister accused Rubio of trying to "instigate a military aggression".

items.title: Angry crowd sets Ebola hospital tents on fire in DR Congo
items.summary: Grieving relatives and friends of a suspected Ebola victim were prevented from taking his body for burial.

items.title: Iran steps up claim to control Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Iran publishes a map claiming "armed forces oversight" across more than 22,000 sq km of the waterway.

items.title: Miner Perpetua Resources secures $2.9 billion U.S. loan for Idaho gold, antimony project
items.summary: Mining company Perpetua Resources has secured a $2.9 billion loan from the U.S. Export-Import Bank as the U.S. looks to secure access to critical minerals.

items.title: Prediction markets are fueling a high-stakes brawl between states and federal regulators
items.summary: Sixteen states are in legal proceedings against prediction market platforms, while one state has moved to ban them.

items.title: SpaceX, OpenAI valuations could mean they leapfrog Berkshire Hathaway on first day of trading
items.summary: Traders on prediction market Polymarket think SpaceX, OpenAI and Anthropic on their first day of trading will surpass valuations of at least $1.4 trillion.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.summary: Rep. items.title: NASCAR’s Kyle Busch, a 2-time champion, dies
items.summary: His family reported that he had been hospitalized with a severe illness.

items.title: House punts Iran war powers resolution vote
items.summary: The House punted a vote Thursday on a Democratic-led war powers resolution aimed at limiting President Trump’s military action on Iran. House Republicans appear to have pushed the vote over attendance issues. Trump and GOP leaders have argued that the president has the unilateral authority to confront Tehran militarily. They’ve also warned that ending the war…

items.summary: Target sees unexpected shift in customer behavior

items.title: Stocks Rally as Crude Oil and Bond Yields Slump

items.title: MIT scientists discover amino acid that helps the gut heal itself
items.summary: MIT scientists have identified cysteine — an amino acid found in foods like meat, dairy, beans, and nuts — as a potent trigger for intestinal repair. In mice, a cysteine-rich diet activated immune cells that released healing signals, helping stem cells rebuild damaged intestinal tissue after radiation exposure. Researchers say the discovery could eventually lead to new dietary therapies for cancer patients suffering from treatment-related gut damage.

items.title: Scientists discover the nutrient that can supercharge cellular energy
items.summary: Researchers discovered that leucine, a nutrient found in protein-rich foods, can supercharge mitochondria by protecting crucial energy-producing proteins inside cells. The breakthrough uncovers a powerful new link between diet and cellular energy — with possible implications for cancer and metabolic disease treatments.

items.title: Common pesticide linked to hidden brain damage, scientists warn
items.summary: Scientists have uncovered alarming new evidence that a common insecticide may leave lasting marks on the developing brain before a child is even born. Researchers studying New York City children found that prenatal exposure to chlorpyrifos — a pesticide once widely used indoors and still used in agriculture — was linked to widespread brain abnormalities and weaker motor skills years later.

items.title: New tools may help diagnose Parkinson’s earlier than ever
items.summary: From special pens to earwax evaluations, a plethora of emerging diagnostics could one day be a major boon for people with the debilitating disease.

items.title: Screening all kids for type 1 diabetes can catch more cases early
items.summary: Identifying children with early signs of type 1 diabetes makes a difference to their health. A new study suggests wider screening is effective.

items.title: One mystery of the Great Pyramid’s longevity has finally been solved
items.summary: Differences in how the pyramid and surrounding soil vibrate, along with design choices, have protected the structure from earthquakes.
